entities.title: Fraud Evaluation
entities.summary: Risk scoring and fraud audit domain.

entities.title: Payments
entities.summary: Core payment checkout and authorization domain.

entities.title: Platform
entities.summary: Cluster provisioning and GitOps operations domain.

entities.title: Checkout Handling
entities.summary:
Checkout handling is the user-facing entrypoint where payment requests are initiated and normalized.
It validates request shape, preserves transaction context, and returns clear customer feedback for each outcome.
It delegates decision logic to payment authorization while protecting the user experience boundary.

entities.title: Cluster Provisioning
entities.summary:
Cluster provisioning creates the runtime substrate used by all application workloads.
It establishes cluster and namespace structure, baseline controls, and environment-level readiness.
This unit is responsible for predictable, repeatable infrastructure foundations.

entities.title: GitOps Operations
entities.summary:
GitOps operations continuously reconciles intended release state with actual runtime state.
It governs rollout flow, drift correction, and operational delivery confidence across payment and fraud workloads.
The unit provides safe and observable release behavior as an ongoing operational capability.

entities.title: Payment Authorization
entities.summary:
Payment authorization orchestrates the final transaction decision path.
It coordinates fraud scoring, external bank interactions, and escalation to support review when needed.
The unit returns deterministic outcomes so downstream behavior remains consistent and testable.

entities.title: Risk Scoring
entities.summary:
Risk scoring computes and classifies transaction risk before approval decisions are finalized.
It provides a stable scoring contract to authorization and supports audit context for later analysis.
The unit is focused on decision quality, consistency, and policy-driven classification behavior.

entities.title: Support Review
entities.summary:
Support review handles manual decisions for escalated or ambiguous payment cases.
It gives support operators context to approve, reject, or request additional verification in a controlled flow.
The unit ensures manual intervention remains auditable, policy-constrained, and operationally reliable.

entities.title: REQ-PAY-001
entities.summary: While normal mode is active, when payment authorization is requested, the payments system shall create a payment session.

entities.title: REQ-PAY-002
entities.summary: Where fraud check is enabled, when payment authorization is requested, the fraud detection system shall calculate a risk score before approval.

entities.title: REQ-PAY-003
entities.summary: If payment is declined, then the payments system shall present a decline reason to customer.

entities.title: REQ-PAY-004
entities.summary: While checkout is active, when payment authorization is requested and risk score is high and risk threshold is exceeded, the payments system shall place the payment in payment review is pending.

entities.title: REQ-PAY-005
entities.summary: Where fraud check is enabled, when payment authorization is requested, the payments system shall persist an audit record with payment id plus risk score.

entities.title: REQ-PAY-006
entities.summary: If bank link is unavailable, then the payments system shall present a temporary unavailable message to customer until bank link is available.

entities.title: REQ-PAY-007
entities.summary: While checkout is active, when payment authorization is requested and 3DS verification is required, the payments system shall request authorization from bank gateway endpoint.

entities.title: REQ-PAY-008
entities.summary: If payment is declined, then the payments system shall present a retry option to customer.

entities.title: REQ-PAY-009
entities.summary: While checkout is active, when payment authorization is requested and risk threshold is exceeded, the payments system shall notify platform operator when support agent is notified.

entities.title: Keep payment authorization separate from checkout handling
entities.summary:
Model checkout handling and payment authorization as separate functional units with explicit
calls and dependency mappings between them.

fields.context:
Checkout handling owns the customer-facing payment journey, while payment authorization owns
transaction decisions that depend on fraud scoring and external bank behavior.
fields.decision:
Model checkout handling and payment authorization as separate functional units with explicit
calls and dependency mappings between them.
consequences: Customer messaging can remain stable while authorization logic changes independently.
Fraud scoring and support review stay closer to authorization decisions than UI flow.
Traceability can distinguish customer experience requirements from transaction decision requirements.
